TO TINE

Corbin- Turner Line and Canadian Pacific Show
Their Hand and Promise Greater Relief to the
Spokane Merchants Than Can Be Secured b

Themselves to any Rairoad-.Wiil Be in Spo-
kane Next Year.

pages.raw_text:

will bind you for any definite period
Of time, for the Canadian Paoihe ana

Before 1905 10 . completed year me
Canadian people win be IN SPOKANE"


Fear lest the business men may win
their fight and in their Joy over .
temporary victory injure their pros
poets for still better things in the
future has led parties closely identi-
fed with Senator Turner and d. c.
Corbin in their railroad project to
make the above statement to mem-
bers Of the shippers association.


This long hoped for information
has come in the last two days not to
one person. but to several, and always
in substantially the same form.


First Definite information.


Because Of the fight being waged
by local shippers for better freight
rates the public now obtains the first
reliable information regarding the
plans of the Canadian Pacific railroad
and the connecting road of Senator
Turner and Mr.. Corbin.


Corbin Raising Funds.


1t is now stated on reliable author
ity that d. C. Corbin, whose name
has long been connected locally with
this new move of the Canadian Pa
cific, is in the east at this time max
ing arrangements to finance the road.
Senator Turner left for the east q
few days ago. ostensibly to try q
case before the supreme court. It
was stated at the time he left that
he would be gone for a month or two.
1t is now learned that after his busi-
ness is completed in Washington city.
he will Join Mr.. Corbin in New York
and together they will push financial
for the new line from
Spokane through the Kootenai coun-
try of northern Idaho to a British
Columbia connection with the Cana
dian Pacific.


For many years Spokane merchants
and jobbers have looked with longing
eyes toward the Independent Canadian
Pacific road. which it is believed will
give Spokane merchants the hong
hoped for rates whenever it secures q
in the city.
